entry.title: Ultimate sales machine
entry.content:
6 steps for Time Management

  1. Touch it once
  2. Making daily lists of the six most important tasks to accomplish
  3. Plan how long each task will be
  4. Assign time slots for accomplishing each task
  5. Focus on the difficult projects first
  6. Ask youself, “Will it hurt me to throw this away?”

Not so many “got-a-minute” meetings,

If your computer signals when you have an email, do not go directly to read it and answer it at the moment

concentration is like a muscle and it strengthens as you concentrate more

Communication levels

  • Trello: schedule features
  • Basecamp + Qortex: client supports
  • Lighthouse: ticket management
  • Github: code management

entry.title: xxxxx
entry.content:
资金走向:

  1. 不同阶层的可支配资金 –> 国有银行 –> 开发商小额贷款建房 –> 用建好的高价房抵押高额贷款 –> 高额建房 (导致房价不断虚高才能购房者填补漏洞)

  2. 银行损失的资金 –> 流入资本市场 –> 白领阶层的购买力 –> 舆论烘托 –> 剩余劳动力填补资金链

  3. 靠 1 –> 2 –> 1 形成闭环，政策在其中归导该环，不至于偏离轨道

房价为什么会涨

  • 在中国，阶层是存在的，不同阶层利益纠纷是存在的。发展中的中国通过通货膨胀和自我改革来维持 可观的GDP, 而每次改革的阵痛不可避免的由下一级的阶层来买单。

  • 体制的僵化跟不上经济的快速发展，垄断经营使矛盾越加激烈。政府通过自我改良不断减少矛盾，但 这注定不是一个简单的过程，既得利益者始终掌握着主要资源，不断收刮剩余价值。

  • 银行竞争下的贷款开放，国有银行却独立运营，开发商通过 贷款 –> 高房价 –> 再贷款 –> 再高房价 的恶性循环把痛苦转嫁给民众或是贷款银行(最终还是普通民众的钱)

  • 中国特色的按揭, 按揭审核宽松是政府为了弥补资金的空洞，补充白领阶层的购买力。

房贷泡沫如何导致白领破产

  • 国内的白领相比国外普遍水平不高，但为了让这一阶层具有一定的购买力，房产商的既得资金会部分 转移到这些领域，致使这领域的白领能够负得起首付，还的起按揭。但全民生产力不提高，却以房产 剥削来的资金并通过资金转移来带动 GDP 增长。

  • 那些年纪轻轻的小白领们，找工作最好把薪水要高一点，别要医疗保险和啥保险，自己把钱存起来最 保险。

  • 你为什么要买车: 因为有时候我觉得去超市买点东西方便，你说这个距离吧，走去不合适，打的不好打。

entry.title: elixir macro
entry.content:
Homoiconic

The property of code being data and data being code is called homoiconicity. The representation of a program is also a data structure in a primitive type of the language.

  • function that takes data as arguments and returns data
  • data can b code representation
  • it’s called at compile time
  • code what it’s invoked will be replaced
  • powerful, performant way to build DSLs
  • Achieve many of the same effects as Metaprogramming but at compile time: Allow us to be lazy about evaluation function calls: arguments are always resolved before they are passed to them

Abstract Syntax Trees(AST)

AST is a tree representation of the syntactical structure of source code. It is referred to as abstract since it does not exactly represent every detail that appears in the real syntax. Since the representation is now focused on the machine and not humans, there may be more optimal modeling that lose some of the details of the source.

  • quote provides functionality to turn code into AST
  • unquote injecte variable values into the AST
  • unquote_splicing turns a list* of arguments into comma delimited arguments
    in the code
  • Macro Escaping:
  • Eval quoted: add a runtime aspect to our compile time macro though with some limited scope
  • hygiene: it’s about scope of your variables
  • unquoting outside of macros: The bind_quoted will disable unquote and thats exactly what we want, it’s clear that unquote is not part of the macro.

====================================

What’s Macro, I think is the code or expression that can be excute during the compilation time …

Quote is commonly used with macro for code generation

Gets the representation of any expression.

Any* Elixir code can be represented using Elixir data structures With some code returns themselves: Atoms, Integers, Floats, Lists, Strings, Two element’s tuple

building code of Elixir macro

At compilation time, squared(5) => 5 * 5

not unquote the same value more than once

Elixir macros are hygienic, variables defining inside macros won’t leak to user context. You can break this by var!

Note that you cannot even access variables defined in the same module unless you explicitly give it a context:

Aliases inside quote are hygienic by default

When defining functions via macros, developers have the option of choosing if runtime errors will be reported from the caller or from inside the quote.

Binding and unquote fragments

  • Unquote fragments provide an easy way to generate functions on the fly

  • This is because the macro is expecting its arguments to be a keyword list at compilation time.

    ┃ kv = [foo: 1, bar: 2] ┃ defkv kv

The code will fail because we are passing the representation of variable kv

In practice, we want to avoid doing work at compilation time as much as possible.

unquote fragment vs regular unquote

By using :bind_quoted, we can automatically disable unquoting while still injecting the desired variables into the tree:

In fact, the :bind_quoted option is recommended every time one desires to inject a value into the quote.

Options:

  • unquote
  • location When set to :keep, keeps the current line and file from quote. Read the Stacktrace information section below for more
  • hygiene
  • context
  • bind_quoted

Examples

entry.title: plug
entry.content:
what’s plug in Elixir

A plug is any function* that receives a connection and a set of options as arguments and returns a tuple in the format { tag :: atom, conn :: Plug.Conn.t }

so it consists of three parts:

  • A Elixir function
  • Receives connection and options
  • Return a tuple { tag :: atom, conn :: Plug.Conn }

If you comes from Ruby you’ll find it similiar to [Rack][rack-link]

…

First try of Plug

Plug let us start with a single file Plug application:

(actually not so single because you need to put in under the plug directory for now since plug is naturally a mix project)

Run: mix run --no-halt first_plug.exs

then navigate to http://localhost:4000 in your any of browser

Adapters

Plug aims to be a common pattern or a bridge attaching the Webservers and applications in Elixir world. And here we would like to see how it connects Cowboy and our single FirstPlug.

Plug.Adapters.Cowboy.http FirstPlug, []

The first argument can be any module which contains call method, we’ll deep into the call method lately.

plug router

The router is a plug, which means it can be invoked as:

Plug.Router compiles all of your routes into a single function and relies on the EVM to optimize the underlying routes into a tree lookup instead of a linear lookup that would instead match route-per-route. That means route lookup are extremly fast in Plug!

defoverridable …

Plug test

plug endpoint

The plug endpoint here is the call method which receives two parameters: Plug.Conn record and Keyword options. Returns can be any atom, with two values have specific meaning to plug:

  • ok – pass the connection to the next plug in the stack
  • halt – halts the current and all other stacks

Any other values means the connection should be halted …

Inside Plug.Connection module, we can see the Plug.Conn was defined with

  • request fields
  • response fields

  • connection fields

  • fetchable fields

  • unfetchable fields

entry.title: elixir notes
entry.content:
Why Mix needs to get dependencies for everty project

hehe

What happens when runnign elixir file.ex

…

What’s different between Elixir and Erlang

The funny thing is that Erlang and Elixir are the same thing under the surface. They “feel” the same to me. — Joe

They both compile instructions(Ruby above 1.9 also has YARV instructions) for the EVM.

  • garbage collection behavior
  • non-shared concurrency model
  • underlying error handling
  • code loading mechanism

are identical …

We have Erlang running on EVM, why we need another language Elixir

We say Erlang here is meant to be the language Erlang, which syntax derived from Prolog and was heavily influenced by smalltalk, CSP.

Elixir combines Erlang and Ruby, has Erlang’s

  • patten matching
  • higher order functions

Ruby’s

  • sigils
  • shortcut syntax

and it’s own(or comes from others)

  • pipe operator
  • macro quote and unquote operators

Elixir underlying AST is kind of different from Erlang one

Elixir also brings a new underlying AST to the table, instead of the Erlang AST where everything form has its own representation, the Elixir AST has a far more uniform representation, which makes meta-programming far easier.

Elixir has mutable variables

Elixir does not have mutable state as you usually find in imperative programming. Lists, tuples and all other data types are immutable. The mutable state you get in Elixir is exactly the same you get in Erlang, nothing less, nothing more.

Rebinding variables makes Elixir unsafe? It could have race conditions?

No, Elixir assignments are compiled down to Static Single Assignment. More importantly, you also can’t change a function’s binding after the function is created.

Elixir breaks a few Erlang holy cows – variables can be re-bound in sequences. This is actually ok, the resulting forms can still be normalized into a static-single-assignment (SSA) form. While this is OK in sequences it would totally verboten-nicht-do-not-do-it in loop constructs. But this is fine, Elixir has no loops, only recursion. Actually it could not have loops with mutable variables since this would be impossible to compile into anything remotely sensible in the EVM. SSA variables in sequences are fine, the EVM knows how to optimize these. Loops no, so Elixir doesn’t go there. It would even boil nicely down into LLVM assembler – but that’s another story too long to tell here. — Joe

What’s “defmacro quote and unquote”

This is parse transforms done right. The joy is that you don’t need to know the abstract syntax. Quote and unquote do the magic for you.

Elixir’s Closures done right VS Javascript’s closure

Outter variable won’t break the closure, so it would always return the same result for the same closure passsin the same parameters.

Why are there two kinds of functions in Elixir

  1. functions defined in module with def
  2. anonymous functions defined with fn, and call with dot+name

Refer to Stackoverflow

  • functions in Elixir are identified by name and arity, that’s why we just use name/arity when using high order functions

  • fn is a closure in Elixir, similar to a lambda in Ruby. we can’t define a closure with different number of arguments.

  • def is named function, which will create a new variable scope we can’t access the outer scoped variable like Closure

  • Use &, we could retrive the named function as an anonymous function

This is also why Elixir uses a dot for calling anonymous functions. Since you can’t simply pass hello around as a function, instead you need to explicitly capture it, there is a natural distinction in between them, and a explicit syntax for calling each makes everything a bit more explicit.

It is not an implementation constraint in the sense that it could also work as f() (without the dot).

Protocols in Elixir

Protocols are mechanism to achive polyorphism in Elixir. It allows us to manipulate any data structure without a need to wrap it or recompile it.

What’s treated false in Elixir

Actually, only false and nil are treated as false. Everything else evaluates to true, even numeric 0, empty list, empty binary.

Built in protocols

  • Access We can always see something like x = [a: 1, b: 2], that’s because Access it the built-in protocols in Elixir

  • Enumerable Any data structures that can be enumerated must implement this protocol.

  • Inspect this protocol is used to transform any data structure into a readable textual representation. This is what tools like IEx use to print results:

  • String.Chars Specifies how to convert a data structure with characters to a string. It’s exposed via the to_string function:

Don’t use exceptions for control-flow, let’s fast fail

Macros

An Elixir program can be represented by its own data structures => building block. like:

{ :sum, [], [1, 2, 3] } => { function, metadata, args }

you could ge the representation of any expression by using the quote:

1 + 2 => {:+, [context: Elixir, import: Kernel], [1, 2]}

sum(1 + 2) => {:sum, [], [{:+, [context: Elixir, import: Kernel], [1, 2]}]}

specially, variables are represented as

x => { :x, [], Elixir }

For more complex expressions, we can see the representation is composed of such nested tuples. Which can be resembled as a tree where each tuple is a node.

Genearal format: { tuple | atom, list, list | atom }

entry.title: stream and enum
entry.content:
enumerator

  • data structures like List, String
  • file

Just calling a function isn’t all that useful for most tasks as there’d be no way to remember previous lines, so some accumulator value is passed to the function and a new accumulator is returned by it.

http://elixir-lang.org/blog/2013/12/11/elixir-s-new-continuable-enumerators/

Just like ActiveRelation in Rails, Elixir make composable, lazy enumeration as streams.

Stream.transform – basic tool

Stream.unfold

Stream.zip

Stream.map

Stream.drop

Stream.each

Stream.each

Stream.after/1

Stream.chunk/4 – parameter step is kind of confusing

Stream.repeatedly vs Stream.iterate vs Stream.resource

IO.stream/1 – Streams input lines, one by one;

URI.query_decoder/1 – Decodes a query string, pair by pair;

entry.title: mix introduction
entry.content:
When we …, we can see … after we pull code from dynamo repo

Get Dynamo dependencies and run tests with: MIX_ENV=test mix do deps.get, test

Running this command, you will fetch dependencies, compile, and then test the framework, make sure you are on the right version with Elixir and Dynamo.

Then runing mix dynamo ../mynamo, Mix will create a directory named mynamo with few files in it:

Note that I can use mix here because mix is an Elixir executable. You can also pass -S mix new ./mynamo as bin/elixir executable’s option

➜ bin/elixir -S mix new ./my_project

bin/elixir

So let’s see what Mix is:

MIX – A BUILD TOOL FOR ELIXIR

  1. Introduction to Mix
  2. Building OTP apps with Mix
  3. Creating custom Mix tasks

Mix is a build tool that provides

  • creating
  • compiling
  • testing
  • releasing (coming soon…)

which ships with every Elixir release.

entry.title: untitled
entry.content:
第一章

  • 信息增长的速度极快，但大部分信息都只是噪声, 噪声增长的速度比信号更快。

  • 预测是人们共同的事业，而不仅仅是那些顶级专家的职责。

  • 我们永远都不可能做出完全客观的预测，因为这些预测都会带有主观色彩。

  • 专注与某一问题的研究总会得到比概括研究更深刻，更独到的见解。

  • 充分理解不可测行和不确定性，理解如何吧信息转变为知识。

在信息时代我们面临的一个重大挑战，就是全球的只是总量在增加，而我们世纪掌握的知识和 自认为掌握的知识之间的鸿沟却越来越宽。

金融危机以及其它大多数失败的预测都源于一种盲目的自信。他们用精确的预测冒充准确的预测。

第二章

entry.title: how ruby excute your code
entry.content:
“YARV is not just a stack machine—it’s a double-stack machine!”

YARV has its own stack pointer and a program counter — that is like your computer’s actual microprocessor.

The CFP pointer indicates the current frame pointer. Each stack frame in your Ruby program stack contains, in turn, a different value for the self, PC, and SP registers,

YARV instruction of puts 2+2

Ruby uses the trace to support the set_trace_func feature.

entry.title: displaying YARV instructions
entry.content:
One easy way to see how Ruby compiles your code is with the RubyVM::InstructionsSequence Object

outputs

As we can see, the line numbers on the left show the position of each instruction the bytecode array that the compiler actually prouces

As we can see, Ruby display the two YARV instruction snippets seperately. The first corresponds to the global scope and the second to the inner block scope.

Here we can see the local table, once Ruby’s ompiler runs, the information about the block parameter is copied out of the AST and into another data structure called the locale table.

entry.title: compilation in Ruby
entry.content:
You may think of Ruby as a dynamic scripting language, but, in fact, it uses a compiler just like C, Java, and myan other programming laungages. Rubys’ compiler works b iterating through the AST produced by the tokenizing and parsing processes, generating a series of bytescode instructions along the way.

The code Ruby actually runs looks nothing like your original code

..

Starting with version 1.9, Ruby compiles your code before executing it.

..

It translates your Ruby code into another language that Ruby’s VM understands The only difference is that you don’t use Ruby’s compiler directly

..

Now the process becomes: tokenizing –> parsing –> compiling

The only differences between YARV and the JVM are the following

  • Ruby doesn’t expose the commpiler to you as a separate tool.

  • Ruby never compiles your Ruby code all the way to machine language

entry.title: use ripper to test ruby tokenization
entry.content:
The code Ruby actually runs looks nothing like your original code.

How Ruby interpret execute your ruby code

process one: tokenize

Run the code you’ll get:

which is a series of tokens converted from our ruby code

process two: parsing

Tokens are grouped into sentences or phrases that make sense to Ruby.

  • LALR parsing algorithm

Ruby optionally dispalys debug information, showing how the parser jumps from one state to another, by using ruby -y simple.rb

AS Ruby parses your code, matching one grammar rule after another, it converts the tokenss in your code file into a comlex interal data structure called an abstract syntax tree

The AST is used to record the structure and syntactical meaning of your Ruby code

entry.title: [Dynamo] Code organization in environments
entry.content:
First of all, Dynamo is a mix project, which shipping with environments independence naturally.

coming soon…

entry.title: 为什么中国有前途
entry.content:
优酷链接

历史跨度: 16, 17 世纪 –> 18, 19 世纪 –> 20 世纪

西班牙, 葡萄牙, 荷兰 –> 日不落帝国 –> 美利坚, 苏联 –> ???

百万级人口 –> 千万级人口 –> 亿级人口

创新 + 扩散 + 规模

边缘国际 –> 中央国家

中国, 印度

战后美国维持近70年世界体系

现有格局影响力

如何看待 自由与民主

铸币税

每个美国老太太 背后都有个 中国老太太

扩展成本 超过了 扩展收益

entry.title: Elixir resources
entry.content:
Elixir is a functional meta-programming aware language built on top of the Erlang VM.
It is a dynamic language with flexible syntax with macros support that leverages Erlang’s
abilities to build concurrent, distributed, fault-tolerant applications with hot code upgrades.

Jose Valim Elixir Homepage

Just as Benjamin said: Having a pretty syntax wrapped around the powerful Erlang VM seems to be an irresistible combination, that’s why Elixir might become the next language you would like to learn :)

Parts of the resources from Elixir wiki

  • Presentation by Alan Peabody

  • Elixir Sips

A twice-weekly screencast series about Elixir by Josh Adams. All screencasts are available for $9 a month. One free episode is available to all as a sample.

  • Elixir love child ruby erlang

This article is a whirlwind tour of some of best features of Elixir

entry.title: One single file rails application
entry.content:
Goodies: * if you find bugs inside rails, you can post as Gist with single file application without a bloated rails application *

config.ru: rackup file => rackup application

rails rackup file:

rails process

  • config/boot.rb # setup the load path
  • config/application.rb # define the application
  • config/environment.rb # initialize the application
  • config.ru # setup rack

In new rails version, we have a Rakefile that just load application file because we don’t need to do the initialization process

example

  • match(get, post, put, delete) # match on the full path
  • mount # match on the path prefix

Summary

The Rack API

PostsController.action(:index)

match vs. mount

Middleware

3 mdiddleware stack * web middleware * rails middleware * controller middleware

Things we got for free

  • middleware
  • before/after action hooks
  • streaming
  • layout & render

Rendering stack

include ActionView::Context

Topics

  • Single-file Rails applications
  • Rack and the Rails router
  • Middleware stack
  • Rails rendering stack

entry.title: Why we choose PDF to post to Evernote
entry.content:
On one of our projects, Evernote is one of the services that users can share data to. Every time users update some data, changes will be posted to Evernote as an Evernote note. But we found some problems when we tried to create an Evernote note via Evernote API.

Problems of creating ENML

ENML is the markup language used by Evernote to represent the content of notes. As a subset of XHTML, ENML has some limitations, for example ENML allows the use of CSS to style notes but all styles must be specified in-line using the ‘style’ attribute. References to external or embedded stylesheets and the use of the ‘class’ attribute are not allowed. Dynamic content generated by Javascript is also disallowed.

The notes we post to Evernote have a kind of complicated structure: HTML documents with an annotated Google map at the bottom. This raises two questions: how could we use existing templates instead of creating another inline-styled ENML versions; and how could we render Google maps inside ENML documents;

We contacted the tech team of Evernote to see if there was any way to help solve the problems, or at least some way to allow us not to convert HTML to inline styling ENML. Unfortunately answers to both questions were no, but one of the developers suggested that we could upload Google maps as attached images and use Evernote Web Clipper, a Chrome extension, to help create ENML from the existing HTML. Although it was not a complete solution, it’s a direction we could try to go in.

Creating images

With the attached images, the first thing that came to my mind was using the static Google maps API. By passing some required parameters, you can get map images from the Google side without worrying how to generate them. But there is a problem: passing parameters is limited to under 1024 bytes which doesn’t suit most of our maps.Therefore, using the Google Maps Javascript API v3 out of browser environment is necessary. One of my colleagues suggested that we could use PhantomJS, a headless WebKit scriptable with a JavaScript API, to help generate the maps images.

PhantomJS is a great tool, but it’s a bit heavy to just create an image from our site, and it makes things more complicated to write some extra Javascript to capture images.

Creating ENML content with the Evernote Web Clipper

The Evernote Web Clipper is a great tool to save anything you like on the internet, including text, links and images. It really helps a lot for most people converting HTML into ENML, but it doesn’t support web fonts very well. Also, maintaining two versions of the template in our system is really not a good choice.

Create PDF as ENML attachment

What we want is a copy of the existing template that we can post to Evernote, instead of ENML content. An image or PDF attachment is acceptable, also Evernote is capable of searching text inside PDFs, which is another big plus. With PDF attachments, we don’t need to think about duplicated documents, inline stylings, web fonts and other issues we met when creating ENML documents. With a simple and popular shell utility wkhtmltopdf in hand, we can easily convert our HTML to PDF easily using the webkit rendering engine.
